$schema_description: 12 brand archetype visual rules (always/never/tend_to). Each rule is structured as an assertion archetype_check.py can evaluate against tokens.json. Sources: Mark & Pearson 'The Hero and the Outlaw' + real brand corpus + references/archetype-do-dont-table.md.

$assertion_grammar.operator: lt | lte | gt | gte | eq | neq | in | not_in | exists | not_exists | between | contains_text | matches_regex
$assertion_grammar.value: RHS value (number, string, list, or [min, max] for between)
$assertion_grammar.rationale: One sentence WHY this rule (for issue messages)
$assertion_grammar._note: Some rules may have severity hint and applies_to_section

Sage.description: Wisdom, knowledge, truth-conveying. Linear, Notion, Stripe partial.

always.rationale: Sage's clarity demands at least WCAG AA contrast
Sufficient breathing room is core to Sage's calm authority
Sage uses one chromatic anchor maximum, restraint is the message

never.rationale: High saturation destroys Sage's calm authority
Decorative gradients undermine Sage's clarity
Large radii feel playful, not sage-like
Sage motion is functional, not theatrical

tend_to.rationale: Sage skews toward cool/neutral palettes
Generous body line-height supports reading-first

Magician.description: Transformation, vision, mystical. Apple keynote, Stripe dark sections.

always.rationale: Magician needs deep darks for transformation atmosphere
At least one chromatic moment must signal transformation

never.rationale: Pure monochromatic loses magical layering
Pure flat design (no shadows, no depth) breaks Magician's mystery

tend_to.rationale: Magician palettes commonly include purple, deep blue, or metallic
Texture differentiation supports magical hierarchy

Hero.description: Courage, mastery, achievement. Nike, Apple Watch, Stripe Connect.

always.rationale: Hero needs at least one strong weight tier for command

never.rationale: Whisper-weight typography lacks Hero's command
Pastel palettes contradict Hero's strength

tend_to.rationale: Hero brands lean toward saturated, decisive color
Hero geometry is sharper, not soft-rounded

Outlaw.description: Rebellion, disruption, anti-establishment. Brutalism, Vimeo (early), Are.na.

never.rationale: Corporate template aesthetic is the opposite of Outlaw
Safe SaaS-blue-purple is what Outlaw rebels against
Polished decoration undermines raw, anti-aesthetic intent

tend_to.rationale: Outlaw often uses raw black/white binaries
Expressive weight contrast over uniform ladder

Explorer.description: Freedom, discovery, ruggedness. Patagonia, Airbnb, REI.

always.rationale: Explorer products are anchored in real-world imagery

never.rationale: Synthetic / over-rendered aesthetics fight Explorer's authenticity

tend_to.rationale: Earth tones (browns, forest greens, ocean blues) common in Explorer
Humanist or serif typefaces for grounded, authentic voice

Creator.description: Imagination, expression, craft. LEGO, Adobe, Figma, Vercel.

always.rationale: Creator products celebrate user-created color content

never.rationale: Creator brand should not constrain user expression

tend_to.rationale: Often uses disciplined neutral chrome to let user content shine (Figma pattern)
Custom or unusual typefaces signal craft awareness

Innocent.description: Optimism, simplicity, kindness. Coca-Cola, Dove, Aveeno, kids products.

always.rationale: Innocent palettes are warm and soft, never harsh
Soft, rounded forms are core to Innocent feel

never.rationale: Severe high-contrast palettes feel harsh, not innocent
Cold geometric sans contradicts Innocent warmth

tend_to.rationale: Pastels and soft tints are signature Innocent palette
Rounded humanist sans or rounded display typefaces

Lover.description: Intimacy, beauty, indulgence. Chanel, Tiffany, Godiva, luxury brands.

always.rationale: Lover designs always breathe — luxury is space

never.rationale: Dense information layouts contradict Lover's indulgence
Geometric-only typography feels mechanical, not sensual

tend_to.rationale: Lover palettes lean warm — beige, dusty rose, dark gold
Serif (especially Didone) typefaces signal Lover's refinement
Hairlines, fine borders, soft shadows

Caregiver.description: Service, nurturing, protection. Johnson & Johnson, UNICEF, Volvo, healthcare.

never.rationale: Aggressive palettes contradict Caregiver's nurture
Severe sharp geometry undermines warmth

tend_to.rationale: Caregiver palettes balance warmth (trust) with cool blues (calm)

Ruler.description: Authority, control, prestige. Rolex, Mercedes, IBM enterprise, Microsoft.

always.rationale: Ruler palettes feature deep blacks, navy, or metallic accents

never.rationale: Casual or playful aesthetic undermines Ruler's authority
Handwritten or quirky typography breaks Ruler's gravitas

tend_to.rationale: Serif or strong geometric sans typefaces
Dark navy / deep black brand color

Jester.description: Joy, playfulness, levity. Old Spice, M&M's, Mailchimp (mature).

never.rationale: Corporate seriousness contradicts Jester's joy
Pure monochromatic feels too restrained for Jester

tend_to.rationale: Multi-color brand palettes are common in Jester
Generous rounded shapes signal playfulness
Elastic / bounce motion supports Jester's joy

Everyman.description: Belonging, accessibility, friendliness. IKEA, Target, Levi's, Slack.

never.rationale: Elite/exclusive aesthetic contradicts Everyman's belonging
Mystery contradicts Everyman's friendliness

tend_to.rationale: Humanist sans-serif (warm, approachable) is the Everyman default
Everyman palettes are friendly, never cold or extreme
